summary: As a Jira Administrator I want to configure user accounts for integration jobs with low login overhead
description: "h3. Problem Definition  _Basic authentication_ for REST API call has a performance overhead. There are a couple of things which contribute to that:  * Authentication itself, also a possible roundtrip to LDAP/AD/Crowd server  * Updating (refreshing) user attributes from LDAP  * Updating login attempt data _recordLoginAttempt_   * Updating UserAttrubutes with updateLastLoginTime  * EventPublishing Security event    h3. Suggested Solution  Add option to configure automation (service) user account which has fewer actions during login (auth):  * Doesn't update Login counter for every successful login (possibly every X requests)  * Doesn't update LastLoginTime for every login  * Doesn't update user attributes from LDAP for every login (example {{updateUserFromRemoteDirectory}} method)     +Possible solution:+  OAuth 2.0 authorization via tokenization should allow for users to self generate a token in order to provide a secure and easy way to integrate their apps at a project level. Tokens should be associated with the corresponding user account limiting permission to the platform based upon the user's access.     h3. Note  Make sure that service account doesn't have Jira Admin privileges. In addition to having a security risk, it also has an extra performance overhead due to processing {{removeDismissFlagForUser}}.    h3. Workaround   Try to avoid _Basic authentication_ for each REST API call, instead try to use _cookie-based authentication_     This is how cookie-based authentication works in Jira at a high level:  * The client creates a new session for the user via the Jira REST API.  * Jira returns a session object that has information about the session including the session cookie. The client stores this session object.  * The client can now set the cookie in the header for all subsequent requests to the Jira REST API.     More details: https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/cookie-based-authentication/"

summary: Site Import / Export
description: "Content Staging is an Enterprise CMS feature that DNN is currently missing. To potentially get to content staging, we're starting with whole-site Import / Export. This will be a stand alone module in Persona Bar > Settings. This may require iPortable v2 and microservices multi-tenant capability. The target content for export is that generated as part of the CMS. Modules that are predominately site visitor/user driven content typically won't take part in this process (Engage modules like Answers, Discussions, Ideas, etc.).     *PERMISSION*  Admin and Host user will have access to this functionality, however Admin will have access only to their own site.    *WHAT ARE WE EXPORTING*  Existing:  * Content  * Files  * Roles  * Profile Properties  * Pages (pick list with option to select/de-select all with a single click)    New:  * Users (each user's membership information: username, password, email, )  * * User profile property values (if users are selected AND profile properties)  * Extension packages (No UI)  ** Modules/Providers/Themes/Language Packs  ** Folder permissions  * HTML Provider Configuration (No UI)  * Vocabularies  * URL's and redirects (No UI)  ** Any AUM settings (site level), any page level redirects  * Evoq page templates (No UI)  * Evoq Connector Data (most of this is probably under portal settings)  * All Portal Settings data (any property from the table should automatically)  ** Anything related to portal alias data will *not* be included  * All Page Settings not currently included, per page  ** This should include any page header, scripts, etc. seen via the page settings UI    *NOT IN SCOPE*  * Page version history  * Asset version history  * Transfer files to production  * Direct site-to-site connection  * Continuous integration  * Analytics data   * Workflow   * Community Module Content (Engage module content, for example)  * Mechanics Activity, Scoring Action or Privilege point data  * Portal Alias data    *EXPORT / IMPORT STEPS*  * Export: The export wizard will involve most of the decision points, in order to produce a final package that is universally accepted and standardized for DNN sites.  * Import: Import should involve less decisions, run without exception, and be a very painless process    *TESTING NOTES*  Upon Export, a .zip package should be created that installs into any other DNN site with similar versions.  "

summary: Implement Moniker API for Service Framework
description: "A moniker is a simple name provided for an objects that simplifies identifying specific object instances.  This concept was first used in Microsoft Object Linking and Embedding (OLE), and the Component Object Model (COM).      The DNN Services Framework was first introduced in DNN 6.2.  When creating web services with the Service Framework, it is common to require callers to provide the ModuleId and TabId as part of the web request header.  This requirement complicates the programming model since service endpoints become tied to their specific location in a website.  Modules are able to easily retrieve the ModuleId and TabId from the service framework as part of the initial module rendering.  However, this mechanism is not available for remote callers, where the UI rendering is not directly controlled by DNN.    In order to encapsulate this context data and to simplify the programming model for web services, we should introduce the concept of a moniker.  The DNN service moniker will provide a mechanism for mapping a simple name to a specific module instance.  This moniker can then be passed to a web service in place of the ModuleId, TabId or any other context data specific to a module.    The moniker should be implemented as an additional system TabModule setting. By default, the moniker will be composed of the page name, the module definition friendly name and the TabModuleId separated by /.  When composing the default moniker, all spaces should be replaced with dashes.  For example, if the Social Groups module was on the “Community” page with TabModuleId of 100, the default moniker would be “Community/Social-Groups/100”.      The default moniker implementation provides a human friendly method to identify the page and module being addressed and also a unique element to differentiate between multiple modules on the same page.  The core platform should expose a service endpoint that allows an application to query for monikers related to a specific module type that the current user has permissions to view.    When calling service endpoints that need a specific module context, the moniker will be included as part of the service call.  The service can then use the moniker to easily lookup any context for the module instance associated with the moniker.    *Developer Notes:*  The purpose of this step is to help the back-end to create the DNN context for the web API request. Therefore, it is not tied to any authentication mechanism (like JWT, Basic, Forms, etc.)    Injecting the moniker in the query part of the API request requires modifying all existing API’s that need to use this moniker  but is not feasible and requires making changes to existing core (which we want to completely avoid). What we need is a non-invasive method. One way in which this can be implemented is by adding a new vendor specific header in the request as an alternate way to using the {{TabID}} and {{ModuleID}} that are in use today. Assume we are going to use {{X-DNN-MONIKER}} for this purpose. An example of a full header would be:  {{X-DNN-MONIKER: John’s Blog}}    Before being able to use this feature, there is a need for an API that returns the various monikers associated with a specific module. Let’s say in an EVOQ Engage site if we need to find all the monikers that use the discussions module, say discussions (i.e., all tabs/pages that have the discussions module in them), we will send an API request that returns all of these monikers associated with discussion modules placed on various pages. This call may return zero, one, or more monikers.    There must be a consideration for user rights in every data returned from the API; no private data should be revealed to an un-authorized user."

summary: Move Liquid Content from Microservice to Platform
description: "We want to move Liquid Content from the Evoq Microservice into modules and extensions within DNN Platform.      Important items to consider:  # Content Library, Content Types, Visualizers and API Keys need to be ported.    # Visualizer module needs to be ported.    # Search Integration  # Database structure/storage     Features not being migrated:  # Content Library -> Facebook, Twitter and LinkedIn Publishing      *Engineering Considerations*  # We need to have proper separation of concerns between web API, business layer and repository layers  # Where possible we should .Net Standard 2.0 class libraries  # Where possible remove dependencies of DNN Dlls  # Must support DNN's native:  ## Permission  ## Localization  ## Workflow   ## Search  # The above systems should be integrated using Dependency Injection  # Must use SQL Server for data storage, but the repository layer should be abstractable, where other data storage (e.g. MongoDB) can be used. Current Liquid Content uses DocumentDB which is Azure only service  ## We may require newer version of SQL Server, e.g. 2016 (especially if we want to take advantage of Json data types)   # Have source code in a new repository outside of Dnn.Platform. Our goal is to keep things as decoupled as possible - continue to slim down Platform, and certainly not more.  ## This should be Library type extension.   ## In theory, one could choose not to install Structured Content on DNN.  ## Any module taking advantage of SC must specify dependency on this library.  # Should be scalable, must support 1M+ content items with ease (of course depends on hardware). Solution should linearly scale with more hardware resources.     "

summary: Support for JIT Gsuite.
description: "With SAML, we added a custom multi-valued user attribute in G Suite as described in the article below to represent groups the user belongs to:https://support.google.com/a/answer/6208725?hl=en    This helped work around the mandatory Groups field in the JIT provisioning settings and allowed us to authenticate successfully with a local account being provisioned automatically. However manually duplicating the list of groups a user belongs to in a custom G suite attribute is a lot of overhead when we consider doing this for hundreds of users. It's also error prone (groups have to be typed in, not selected from a list when adding to the custom attribute) and is also a security risk (eg: not removing the group name from the custom attribute when the user's been removed from the Google group could result in a user still having access to resources they're not supposed to have access to). We also reached out to Google to see whether there's a way to fetch the actual Google groups as an attribute, however they said it's not possible at this time and that the application needs to fetch the group information using the Directory API (please see here: https://developers.google.com/admin-sdk/directory/v1/reference/groups/list). Is supporting G Suite as an IdP in the SSO settings using this mechanism something we can request as a feature?"

summary: Provide support for multiple identity providers (IdPs) in native DC SAML connector
description: "h3. Problem Definition  The native SAML connector in Jira Data Center only allows for the configuration of a single IdP. This is limiting for certain use cases, especially where external users may be authenticating to an instance via a different domain.     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide support for configuration multiple IdPs using the native SAML connector.     h3. Workaround   There are several third-party DC SAML/SSO plugins on the Atlassian Marketplace that allow for the configuration of multiple IdPs."

summary: Modifying published catalogs and tables in Qserv
description: "The present implementation of the Replication/Ingest system's management system already supports deleting partially ingested catalogs (not tables) using the following resource:  ||method||resource||  |DELETE|/ingest/database/:name|    Unfortunately, the present mechanism leaves behind a number of other use cases which are still important. Hence the *main* objective of the proposed effort is to extend the existing implementation of the system's management interface (the REST API) to allow deleting select tables from the *non*-{{published}} catalogs as well as tables from the {{published}} catalogs, or the whole catalogs. These are examples of catalogs which may need to be deleted:   * test catalogs   * obsolete or wrong (based on the incorrect input) catalogs   * catalogs for which a new (better) version had been ingested   * less important catalogs (to clear disk space in Qserv workers before ingesting more important ones)   * catalogs contributed by users if a users is n longer interested in the catalog    The *second* objective of the effort is to implement a mechanism for *un-{{publishing}}* catalogs. In addition to the above stated scenarios, this one would also allow _extending_ existing catalogs with more tables, _chunks_, adding rows to existing _regular_ or _partitioned_ (_chunked_) tables, removing rows (based on a specific {{super-transaction}}) from existing _regular_ or _partitioned_ (_chunked_) tables. The proposed _bi-directional_ transitions of catalogs between the {{published}} and *non-*{{published}} states would be needed for   * QA-ing large catalogs during their _incremental_ ingests   * fixing problems with catalog ingests which were discovered during QA. This would be done by removing contributions into the catalog made during a specific {{super-transaction}}   * supporting user-level (contributed) catalogs which may be less static by their very nature compared with the production ones (LSST DRP, etc.)    Additional requirements/restrictions:   * all operations affecting *non-*{{published}} catalogs should begin with *un-*{{publishing}} a catalog first.   * the operations affecting the {{published}} catalogs will require adding extra security (management privileges) on top of what was already implemented with the {{auth_key}} based authorization: https://jira.lsstcorp.org/browse/DM-22745. One option here which won't require changing the protocol would be to discriminate different values of the keys to treat some of those as the ""high privilege"" ones.   * the operation should *not* allow deleting the {{director}} table alone unless the whole database is being deleted.    Deleting a table requires eliminating the following data within Qserv and the Replication system's configuration:   * all instances of the table at workers   * an instance of the table from the {{information_schema}} table at Qserv {{czar}}'s database   * the the corresponding entry from {{CSS}}   * the table entry from the Replication System's Configuration    Deleting a whole catalog (database) requires deleting:   * all instances of the database at workers   * an instance of the database from the {{information_schema}} table at Qserv {{czar}}'s database   * the the corresponding entry from {{CSS}}   * the {{secondary_index}} for the {{director}} table of the database   * the {{empty chunk list}} file or the table.   * the database entry from the Replication System's Configuration    The proposed operation of *un*-{{publishing}} a catalog requires:   * eliminating the catalog from {{CSS}} to prevent Qserv from seeing the catalog   * reinstalling MySQL partitions (or creating the default partition *0* if none existed before) at all tables of the catalog. This step should also affect the {{secondary index}}    The the following resource are to be added:  ||method||resource||role||  |DELETE|/ingest/table/:name|deleting select tables of the *non*-{{published}} catalogs|  |PUT|/replication/database/:name|*un*-{{publish}} a catalog, so that it could be further modified|"

summary: Investigate alternatives for the Parquet Sink connector
description: "One of the output formats required for the interoperability of the EFD and the LSP is Parquet. Parquet files have proven to be an efficient columnar data source for large datasets and also work with Dask distributed processing.    There are some other requirements for the Parquet Sink connector.   * We want to use Kafka Connect as for the other EFD connectors   * It must be open-source, if it is provided by Confluent it must be under the [Confluent Community Licence| https://www.confluent.io/confluent-community-license] since we are not paying for the Confluent Enterprise License.   * It must support time-based data partitioning, we want to split the EFD data by observing night.   * Must be able to read from Avro   * Should write to POSIX file system or to an object-store.     "

summary: Extend Qserv Replication system to allow exporting tables/chunks
description: "h1. Objectives  The proposed functionality of the Qserv Replication System would allow an efficient mechanism for:  * exporting (ready to be ingested) data for external users, LSST sites, or Qserv instances of the same site  * backing up catalog data  * _in-place_ re-partitioning and re-ingesting catalogs if a change in the partitioning configuration was required    This functionality would be especially useful (or be the only option) if the original (source) of data used for ingesting a catalog is either not-available, or it's difficult to use for (re-)ingesting the catalog.    h1. Implementation details  Here is a summary of changes/additions to the existing code:  * extending system's Configuration service to allow specifying an internal (at workers) _export_ directory where the *{{CSV}}*/*{{TSV}}*-formatted dumps of the tables/chunks would be temporarily stored before returned them to clients/requestors  * adding the _export_ service to be run at each Replication System's worker  * adding the client API (library) for interactions with the workers _export_ services  * adding a binary applications build upon the API for retrieving the data from the _export_ services and writing them as local files  * extending the REST API of the *Master Replication Controller* for retrieving connection parameters of the above mentioned _export_ services needed to retrieve a content of a _regular_ table or a _chunk_ (or _chunks_) of a _partitioned_ table  * unit and/or integration tests where it may seem feasible"

summary: Integration tests for channel participation API
description: "Integration tests for the channel participation API.   - Create test helpers that create and invoke REST API calls on server.  (/)    *Test cases*    With a system channel:   * Test list, list all, of network with system channel, w solo/kafka/ & raft. On raft: with multiple orderers & multiple channels, not all orderers on all channels. (/)   * Test the negatives of a network with a system channel - cannot join, cannot delete an app channel.   (/)    Without a system channel (raft only):   * Test join: as member, follower, with & without on-boarding.   (/)   * Test join: switch between member & follower and vise versa.   (/)   * Test remove: remove app channels. Add and remove same channel multiple times.  (/)   * Verify the effects of join & remove with list & list all   (/)    Test crash fault tolerance:   * On-boarding: when the orderer crashes before the follower reaches the join-block (i.e. before it was deleted from the file repo)   * Following: when the orderer crashes after the follower reaches the join-block (i.e. it was already deleted form the file repo)   * Remove: orderer crashes while removing a (big) channel (i.e. before removing the marker form the file repo - not sure how to crash the orderer in the right time for this...)    Transitions between system-channel-style & API style   * Test create system channel on empty network, continue to create channels the old way.   * Test removal of system channel on a network with channels, continue to manage with channel participation API.  (/)"

summary: JIRA REST API with JWT authentication
description: "We can confirm after a conversation with the DEV team, there is *no support* for server querying of the *JIRA REST API* with *JWT* authentication.      The DEV team is working to implement *oAuth2.0* for the Jira server, but currently, only *Jira cloud* has support for *JWT*.    (?) Can we have a feature that makes Jira consume JWT token for REST APIs usage?    Something similar to the JWT authentication which is being used on Jira Cloud, but, in preference something more ""generic"" that users can integrate with their own apps.    Feature Request created based on [PS-66118|https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/PS-66118]    We can see it's implemented for Confluence as well: [https://developer.atlassian.com/server/confluence/How-to-make-REST-API-endpoints-available-for-JWT-authentication/]"

summary: Provide support for multiple identity providers (IdPs) in native DC SAML connector
description: "h3. Problem Definition  The native SAML connector in Jira Data Center only allows for the configuration of a single IdP. This is limiting for certain use cases, especially where external users may be authenticating to an instance via a different domain.     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide support for configuration multiple IdPs using the native SAML connector.     h3. Workaround   There are several third-party DC SAML/SSO plugins on the Atlassian Marketplace that allow for the configuration of multiple IdPs."

summary: User creation & update on-the-fly with data from SAML attributes
description: "Currently, while configuring SAML with Jira, it is required using an User Directory (internal or LDAP) to manage application access and such configuration must be done upfront, as there is no automated way to synchronize users between internal directories and the SAML identity provider (as described [here|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/adding-saml-integration-to-your-existing-user-management-infrastructure-861244976.html#AddingSAMLintegrationtoyourexistingusermanagementinfrastructure-Usingyourapplication'sinternaldirectory]).    The suggestion here is to create this automation, where user creation and update can be done automatically while log in Jira using SAML.  "

summary: Implement a new component to launch CSI plugins as standalone containers and make CSI gRPC calls
description: "*Background:*    Originally we want `volume/csi` isolator to leverage the existing [service manager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/service_manager.hpp#L50:L51] to launch CSI plugins as standalone containers and currently service manager needs to call the following agent HTTP APIs:   # `GET_CONTAINERS` to get all standalone containers in its `recover` method.   # `KILL_CONTAINER` and `WAIT_CONTAINER` to kill the outdated standalone containers in its `recover` method.   # `LAUNCH_CONTAINER` via the existing [ContainerDaemon|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/container_daemon.hpp#L41:L46] to launch CSI plugin as standalone container when its `getEndpoint` method is called.    The problem with the above design is, `volume/csi` isolator may need to clean up orphan container during agent recovery which is triggered by containerizer (see [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/containerizer/mesos/containerizer.cpp#L1272:L1275] for details), to clean up an orphan container which is using a CSI volume, `volume/csi` isolator needs to instantiate and recover the service manager and get CSI plugin’s endpoint from it (i.e., service manager’s `getEndpoint` method will be called by `volume/csi` isolator during agent recovery. And as I mentioned above service manager’s `getEndpoint` may need to call `LAUNCH_CONTAINER` to launch CSI plugin as standalone container, since agent is still in recovering state, such agent HTTP call will be just rejected by agent. So we have to instantiate and recover service manager *after agent recovery is done*, but in `volume/csi` isolator we do not have such information (i.e. the signal that agent recovery is done).    *Solution*    We need to implement a new component (like `CSIVolumeManager` or a better name?) in Mesos agent which is responsible for launching CSI plugins as standalone containers (via the existing [service manager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/service_manager.hpp#L50:L51]) and making CSI gRPC calls (via the existing [volume manager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/volume_manager.hpp#L55:L56]).   * We can instantiate this new component in the `main` method of agent and pass it to both containerizer and agent (i.e. it will be a member of the `Slave` object), and containerizer will in turn pass it to the `volume/csi` isolator.   * Since this new component relies on service manager which will call agent HTTP APIs, we need to pass agent URL to it, like `process::http::URL(scheme, agentIP, agentPort, agentLibprocessId + ""/api/v1"")`, see [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L459:L471] for an example.   * When agent registers/reregisters with master (`Slave::registered` and `Slave::reregistered`), we should call this new component’s `start` method (see [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L1740:L1742] and [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L1825:L1827] as examples) which will scan the directory `--csi_plugin_config_dir` and create the `service manager - volume manager` pair for each CSI plugin loaded from that directory.   * For the `volume/csi` isolator, it needs to call this new component’s `publishVolume` and `unpublishVolume` methods in its `prepare` and `cleanup` method.    In the case of clean up orphan containers during agent recovery, `volume/csi` isolator will just call this new component’s `unpublishVolume` method as usual, and it is this new component’s responsibility to only make the actual CSI gRPC call after agent recovery is done and agent has registered with master (e.g., when this new component’s start method is called)."

summary: Provide HTTP authenticatee interface re/usable for the scheduler library.
description: "h4. Motivation  Authentication and authorization have been added to most Mesos APIs at this point. Schedulers making use of the Mesos HTTP scheduler library however, currently only support a hard wired basic HTTP authentication.  To secure the master’s HTTP scheduler API, the {{/api/v1/scheduler}} endpoint must be authenticated. Without authentication, a malicious or buggy actor from within or outside the cluster could send requests to these master endpoints, potentially disrupting running schedulers or tasks, injecting harmful tasks, or exposing privileged information.   h4. Goals  - Support custom authentication of schedulers based on the Mesos V1 HTTP scheduler API library [/src/scheduler/scheduler.cpp|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/8198579fea7e433e202bd33f4ea62eb235859365/src/scheduler/scheduler.cpp]. - Require minimal operator configuration when enabling scheduler authentication for a simple default use case. - Provide a thin, reusable layer of abstraction enabling any HTTP API consumer to authenticate. "

summary: SDK OAuth support
description: "Tracks the effort related to supporting extensions which use OAuth as an authentication method"

summary: Webpack: Integration into the CLI build command
description: "To integrate Webpack support in the CLI we need the following changes to our build:    - A new hook that communicates with appcd-plugin-webpack to get webpack build results.  - Assets generated by Webpack need to be excluded from our existing JavaScript processing from node-titanium-sdk.  - Bootstrap files will be loaded dynamically during runtime. These files need to be loaded through a [dynamic expression in import()|https://webpack.js.org/api/module-methods/#dynamic-expressions-in-import].  - (Optional) Provide the raw sources of our common JavaScript sources. Currently we bundle all files and the deprecated {{@babel/polyfill}} via rollup to create the {{ti.main.js}}. This could be optimized through a seperate webpack entry point which would allow to load seperate polyfills based in our internal usage, and end-user code usage."

summary: TiAPI: Add support for Promises
description: """The modern development world is highly asynchronous"".    I agree, so we should add support for promises that can be used from the JS world to improve the general callback structure. On the native side, we should a new class ""TiPromise"" that can be used in the SDK, native modules and Hyperloop to allow promises.    I am not completely sure about the architecture so far, but we should definitely talk about it!"

summary: Remove artificial ack timeout values for messages sent by Archivers
description: "When we send a message from the archiver to the forwarder, we expect to get back an ACK for that message.  We also set a timeout value for that ACK. If we don’t get the ACK after the timeout expires, we go into a fault state.  Note that the ack timeout is there to detect that something really bad is happening and that the system likely won’t get an ack no matter how long it waits.       What happened last week was that Michael sent a lot of requests to read BIAS information in very quick succession, with a very small delay. This causes messages to be backed up on the RabbitMQ queue since messages aren’t read until the work is ready to be started.  Since the initial message was sent, and a timeout was started, the timeout expires even before the Forwarder gets it from the message queue.       The simplest (and IMO, wrong) way to prevent this is to increase the ack timeout value in the config file. This is only a short term solution and pushes the problem off. We know this because we’ve already done this once; the ack timeout used to be 10 seconds, and now it’s 60. Increasing the ack timeout just kicks the can down the road.       There are several ways to fix this, and Htut-Khine and I went over several ideas.  Messages could be immediately retrieved in a thread, and then kept locally (or in a Redis database) after being acknowledged. One thread could keep reading and filling the queue, another (or multiple threads) reads from the work queue does the work. Note that this kind of defeats the purpose of the original ack timeout, since messages are responded to quickly, masking an error where the work wasn’t actually done.      We do have another mechanism in place to detect that the Forwarder is gone, which we also currently use, so we might be able to eliminate the timeout completely.  If that mechanism detects that the Forwarder is gone, the Archiver will go into a fault state. (The Forwarder can also detect when the Archiver is gone).      Note that we would still send ACKs back; we just wouldn’t time out, eliminating the artificial backlog response issue. We would also still keep the “aliveness” mechanism in place, so if the Forwarder crashed, we’d know and go into a fault state.      I think this means we can safely eliminate the ack timeout."

summary: Add abstractions for spatial indexing to daf.butler.registry.Database
description: "Our current approach to spatial indexing on the sphere is simply to store the HTM IDs each region overlaps at a particular level, in an extra ""common skypix overlap"" table for every table that has a region column.  This might be an okay approach for regions of a certain size, but it's definitely not a good approach for regions of arbitrary size, and probably not close to the best approach even for its ideal region size.  Doing better would probably require using special index types built for spatial overlap queries (R trees and variants thereof).  While the databases of interest to us (SQLite, Oracle, and PostgreSQL) do not support indexing regions on the sphere natively, they all do provide ways to index 3-d bounding boxes, and the lsst.sphgeom objects we work with provide ways to get 3-d bounding boxes (for a unit sphere).  The interfaces for the three databases are quite different, however, so the big challenge is probably encapsulating that functionality behind a consistent interface for the rest of our Registry code to use.  We might be able to leverage SQLAlchemy hooks for some of this customization, but I doubt they will cover everything.  While Oracle and PostgreSQL both treat geometries as column types and spatial indexes much like normal indexes, they have different syntax for inserting, querying, and relating them.  SQLite is even more different: its R tree implementation involves creating a special table for every spatial column, in which lower and upper bounds behave like regular (but separate) columms.  Adding at least some of the abstraction to our Database class might be a lot easier, and no less usable, though at present it doesn't do much with SELECT queries (they're just passed through to SQLAlchemy directly).  And I don't think this functionality needs to be totally transparent - just database-generic.  It would be totally reasonable for Registry code that's using our Database interface to work with a spatial column to have to do things it wouldn't have to do with a normal column, like manually extracting bounding boxes from regions for inserts, calling Database methods to get SQLAlchemy expressions that represent overlaps in query join/where clauses, or doing post-query filtering in Python (as we do now).  This ticket is for adding that abstraction layer to some combination of SQLAlchemy and the Database class.  I'll create another one for actually using it in Registry (maybe after the design for this one is further along). "

summary: Test improvements - Replace Behave Tests
description: "Deprecate Behave test tool framework after providing equivalent IT coverage with other test tools and test suites.    Behave framework, in fabric-test repository, is used for numerous tests to test the innerworkings of fabric-test (smoke test suite) and the functionality and interworking of other repositories (interop and daily test suites). With minimal resources available that have the python knowledge and experience to maintain the tests, which are simple to read but difficult to quickly understand and fix, the intent is to eliminate the use of the Behave framework for any Fabric projects.    Work items herein are to evaluate the red and yellow highlighted testcases in the attached spreadsheet, which lists all the active (and some inactive) Behave testcases in the fabric-test/feature/*.feature files.  We can eliminate those which are duplicates of other tests being executed by PTE or other tools inside fabric-test repository or in other IT tests in other repositories such as fabric or fabric-sdk-java or fabric-sdk-node or others. Where there are gaps, and where the test coverage is deemed still necessary, new workitems should be created to implement them elsewhere.    The attached xls spreadsheet is a snapshot after first pass performed by Matt and Scott. Follow the ongoing design analysis work at the link provided in the ""Design"" section. For each testcase, confirm whether it is covered elsewhere or if it is a gap. If a gap, indicate if it is needed and if so then which test suite - i.e. when it should be executed in the pipeline, and the location where a replacement test will be developed - and create a jira issue for writing each single or group of tests.    A good goal is to replace these tests and deprecate the Behave test framework before the end of 2019, when python v2.7 will become unsupported (which means we would need to invest some effort to upgrade our tooling just to keep it going).    Procedural recommendations:    For end-to-end behavior testing, use fabric integration tests.  We don't want to proliferate too many integration tests, so for negative testing on a specific component, use unit tests.  Note - it is acceptable for a unit test to span more than one package of code to test a couple implementation layers at a time (you don't have to mock out every single layer).  For tests that use SDK, work with Bret/Rick to extend SDK integration tests.  Note - we do not expect full coverage of Fabric logic from the SDK integration tests. We expect full coverage of SDK code and integration with the important Fabric functions only.    Additional test efficiency suggestions:: Streamline existing lifecycle integration tests so that they run faster - For example, instead of having one network that you bring up and down per test, try to do more testing using fewer networks. e.g. start one network and then create a channel per unique test sequence. Or combine multiple test scenarios on a single network channel."

summary: Improve migration integration test
description: "see comments:    https://gerrit.hyperledger.org/r/c/fabric/+/31856    - remove chaincode/peer from exiting kafka->raft & solo->raft IT,  use in-proc client instead of chaincode/peer.    - expand the network from single node to 2-node kafka->raftafter solo->etcdraft transition"

summary: Filter sensitive information leaked through JMX monitoring
description: "There are cases where system accounts and passwords (in clear text) are reported via JMX beans. One example is in the database connector bean. Accessing JMX does require a password, but we did not realize that read-only JMX access may be exposing system-level accounts.    Access to the JMX metrics might be allowed to both monitoring tools and custom plugin developers for debugging. Some beans contain passwords that shouldn't be distributed, for example, the DB Connector Bean shows the database username/password as a ""metric"" in the bean"

summary: Ability to have the Websudo functionality working with SAML / SSO
description: "h3. Problem Definition  When implementing SAML either through JDC or through a vendor plugin, the net result is you have to turn off websudo because you can't get websudo and SAML to work. The effect is you can go straight into administration functions without confirmation that you should. This poses a security risk.     h3. Suggested Solution  I am requesting an enhancement to allow websudo to work with both your JDC SAML setup as well as to allow websudo to work with other marketplace SAML plugins."

summary: Provide a way to whitelist IPs for REST API basic authentication
description: "h3. Problem Definition  * Currently Jira doesn't provide a way to restrict or whitelist source IPs (JRASERVER-67452) for remote API interaction at all. One of the challenges of implementing a workaround for this at proxy/network environment level is that the REST API URL patterns for the public API are also often used by requests triggered from the UI. So there is a risk of blocking normal UI based requests while intending to block remote API calls.    h3. Suggested Solution  One way to constrain remote API usage to some extent may be to limit or restrict the use of basic auth for REST API. Provide facility or configuration to restrict basic authentication requests to whitelisted IPs."

summary: Option to add custom headers to be sent in Jira webhook
description: "h3. Problem Definition  Often when integrating Jira with other remote applications or services using webhook, there is a requirement from the remote application to provide certain headers  e.g. csrf protection tokens for security or authentication purposes. Jira webhooks provides no way to send custom headers.      h3. Suggested Solution  Provide a way to supply custom header and value in the webhook callback configuration, similar to the feature provided in Jira Service Desk automation webhooks.    h3. Workaround  If your integration is with a Jira Service Desk project, you might be able to send your webhooks using a SD automation rule instead, this allows sending custom headers in the POST request."

summary: Support v1 Executor API over gRPC.
description: "Supporting v1 Executor API over gRPC will enable people to write custom executors with more language bindings. The main work includes:   1. Define the Executor gRPC service in {{service.proto}}. The proto will be in proto3, but the request and response messages will still be in proto2, since this is what the current v1 API based on.   2. Refactor the agent code to support both HTTP and gRPC connections to reuse most of the current code for HTTP Executor API.   3. Implement handlers for gRPC calls."

summary: Support v1 Operator Master streaming API over gRPC.
description: "Supporting v1 Operator Master streaming API over gRPC will enable people to interact with Mesos through more language bindings, and the UI can be based on gRPC. The main work includes:  1. Add the streaming calls to the gRPC service in {{service.proto}}. The proto will be in proto3, but the request and response messages will still be in proto2, since this is what the current v1 API based on.  2. Refactor the master code to support both HTTP and gRPC connections to reuse most of the current code for HTTP Operator streaming API.  3. Implement handlers for gRPC calls."

summary: Support v1 Scheduler API over gRPC.
description: "Supporting v1 Scheduler API over gRPC will enable people to write frameworks with more language bindings. The main work includes:   1. Define the Scheduler gRPC service in {{service.proto}}. The proto will be in proto3, but the request and response messages will still be in proto2, since this is what the current v1 API based on.   2. Refactor the master code to support both HTTP and gRPC connections to reuse most of the current code for HTTP Scheduler API.   3. Implement handlers for gRPC calls."

summary: Allow framework to opt-in to forward executor's JWT token to the tasks
description: "Nested container API is an awesome feature and enabled a lot of interesting use cases. A pattern we have seen multiple times is that a task (often the only one) launched by default executor wants to further creates containers nested behind itself (or the executor) to run some different workload.  Because the entire request is 1) completely local to the executor container, 2) okay to be bounded within the executor's lifecycle, we'd like to allow the task to use the mesos agent API directly to create these nested containers. However, it creates a problem when we want to enable HTTP executor authentication because the JWT auth tokens are only available to the executor so the task's API request will be rejected.  Requiring framework owner to fork or create a custom executor simply for this purpose also seems a bit too heavy.  My proposal is to allow framework to opt-in with some field so that the launched task will receive certain environment variables from default executor, so the task can ""act upon"" the executor. One idea is to add a new field to allow certain environment variables to be forwarded from executor to task."

summary: Support gRPC client
description: "CSI support requires gRPC client integration in Mesos. This epic captures the work that needs to be done for supporting that.  1. Bundle GRPC     - CMake build system under Linux.     - Setting up autotools under Linux.     - Setting up CMake build system under Windows.     - Testing on other supported OSes, e.g., macOS.  2. Build a GRPC client API in libprocess     - This should be a wrapper over the grpc client that integrate with libprocess's event loop so that caller will be much easier to consume."

summary: HTTP Authenticator modules should be able to redirect users
description: "RIght now, Autheticator modules can only respond with an Unauthorized HTTP status code if they need to get auth information from the client. This works for Basic auth, but not for authentication types like oauth, which expect the server to redirect the client to the right authorization provider URL.  We should change AuthenticationResult to allow arbitrary http responses to allow for more flexibility here."

summary: Implement a JWT HTTP authenticatee module
description: "An implementation of the new {{HttpAuthenticatee}} interface should be added for executors to use when authenticating with the default JSON web token (JWT) authenticator module. This module will be loaded into the default executor by default when HTTP executor authentication is enabled."

summary: Provide Hybrid authentication/authorization capability
description: "Provide a way to allow a Hybrid authentication/authorization model. Such as using SAML for authentication, and use LDAP for authorization (group mapping)."

summary: Tycho Unzip Plugin for Nexus 3
description: "As requested by <USER>in NEXUS-10495, here are the functional requirements that the Tycho Unzip plugin (currently based on Nexus 2.x) fulfills:    * Detailed description see https://wiki.eclipse.org/Tycho/Nexus_Unzip_Plugin  * Sources: https://github.com/eclipse/tycho.nexus  * it provides virtual repositories (on top of hosted as well as group repositories) that give transparent access to files inside zip/jar archives as if they were extracted  * you can browse inside zip/jar files by adding suffix ""-unzip"" to the URL  * You can see it live here:  https://repo.eclipse.org/content/unzip/  e.g. repo group   https://repo.eclipse.org/content/groups/releases/org/eclipse/egit/org.eclipse.egit.repository/4.6.0.201612231935-r/  can be viewed as unzipped using  https://repo.eclipse.org/content/unzip/releases.unzip/org/eclipse/egit/org.eclipse.egit.repository/4.6.0.201612231935-r/org.eclipse.egit.repository-4.6.0.201612231935-r.zip-unzip/  note that dir listing inside the zip works as if the zip was extracted.  * as an option useVirtualVersion=true, latest SNAPSHOT artifacts can be accessed without having to know the date of the latest SNAPSHOT, e.g.  although there are many SNAPSHOTs in  https://repo.eclipse.org/content/groups/snapshots/org/eclipse/egit/org.eclipse.egit.repository/4.7.0-SNAPSHOT/  you could access the latest SNAPSHOT using  https://repo.eclipse.org/content/groups/snapshots/org/eclipse/egit/org.eclipse.egit.repository/4.7.0-SNAPSHOT/org.eclipse.egit.repository-4.7.0-SNAPSHOT.zip-unzip  (this option is not enabled on eclipse.org)        Depending on how much has changed in the Nexus plugin architecture from Nexus version 2 to 3, a rewrite based on the requirements may be easier than trying to port the existing plugin.    If there would be a drop-in replacement rewrite for Nexus 3 provided by Sonatype, this plugin would also have to be open source under EPL or Apache 2.0 License (otherwise e.g. the Eclipse Foundation won't be able to use it). Existing unzip URLs to p2 repos on Nexus are hardcoded in many places, so compatibility on URL level would be important.    This is also tracked at eclipse:  https://bugs.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=498124  "

summary: Inconsistent Android SDK/NDK and JDK detection logic
description: "This is an internal development and architecture issue.    We have 3 separate ""codebases"" around detecting the Android SDK/NDK and the JDK/JAVA_HOME.    For developers building our SDK, we have some logic I wrote up in our ""scons"" scripts: https://github.com/<USER>titanium_mobile/tree/master/build/lib/android    For SDK users (Titanium/Appc CLI) we delegate to node-titanium-sdk: https://github.com/<USER>node-titanium-sdk/blob/master/lib/android.js  Which also delegates the jdk/Java stuff to node-apps: https://github.com/<USER>node-appc/blob/master/lib/jdk.js    And for the Daemon, it uses androidlib and jdklib:  - https://github.com/<USER>androidlib/tree/master/src  - https://github.com/<USER>jdklib/tree/master/src  (And although not explicit, it really also uses https://github.com/<USER>appcd-plugin-jdk/blob/master/src/jdk-info-service.js to scan Windows registry, etc)    We should not keep re-inventing the wheel repeatedly here. We should be re-using the same logic to detect these pieces regardless of whether we are building the SDK, using the SDK or using the daemon."

summary: Ability to have the Websudo functionality working with SAML / SSO
description: "h4. Problem Definition    When using JIT provisioning in Confluence Data Center, you can't access Confluence Administration because the websudo form gives a *wrong credentials* error.  h4. Suggested Solution    I am requesting an enhancement to allow websudo to work with SAML setup as well as to allow websudo to work with other marketplace SAML plugins.  h4. Workaround    There are two possible workarounds to access Confluence Administration:   * *Disable the secure sessions*, removing the websudo form. As a side-effect, you can go straight into administration functions without confirmation that you should. This poses a security risk.   * *Set a new password for the user that was created with JIT provisioning*: JIT provisioning creates a user in Confluence Internal Directory and you can define a password for this user. As a side-effect, an administrator will have 2 passwords - the IdP password to log in Confluence and the Internal password to authenticate in secure-sessions.    h4.  "

summary: As a Confluence Administrator I should be able to enable a System App (or a module) from the UPM UI if it is currently disabled
description: "h3. Problem Definition    Sometimes a System App, or a module of a System App, might be disabled while Confluence is running or during startup. This could happen by a bug on Confluence or modified directly from the database.   A Confluence administrator isn't able to enable this App/Module from the Universal Plugin Manager UI, requiring some downtime to restart the application when the problem is identified.    Note that this enhancement is not targeted on being able to disable these Apps/modules, but solely on the ability to *enable* them.  h3. Suggested Solution    When a System App (or module of a system app) is disabled, give the Confluence Administrator the ability to enable it from the UPM UI.  h3. Workarounds  h4. Use UPM REST API  Call UPM REST API to change the status of a given plugin or a single module  * [UPM REST API|https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/UPM/pages/6094960/UPM+REST+API]    h4. Directly updating the database  Enable the App or the module via data manipulation on the database.  Refer to the following KB articles on how to accomplish it:   * [How to Manually Change a Plugin Back to Default State Through the Database|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-change-a-plugin-back-to-default-state-through-the-database-592609333.html]   * [How to reset all Confluence plugins back to their default state through the database|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-reset-all-confluence-plugins-back-to-their-default-state-through-the-database-859509873.html]   * [How Do I find enabled and disabled plugins in the database?|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/how-do-i-find-enabled-and-disabled-plugins-in-the-database/]"

summary: REST API Endpoint for Content Indexing Queue pending items
description: "For monitoring purposes, it would be helpful to have an endpoint that would return the output of the page http://<confluence>/admin/viewindexqueue.action, focusing the output to the amount of pending indexing items in the queue.    That'd help customers have visibility of their index queue in a proactive way, having them take measures when that queue is increasing and not decreasing a given period of time."

summary: Add support for Apple SSO login methods to native iOS Confluence Server app
description: "We support SSO login as long as the login that is being done inside the in-app web-view is successful. We can see that the for login to work the Apple-specific SSO {{AppleConnect}} extension is needed, which works with iOS Safari browser. The login fails because the in-app web-view is built from Apple's {{WKWebview}} which may not support AppleConnect extension.    The mobile app can solve this by using {{SFSafariViewController}} (even though it's deprecated) within the app, which will allow our Connect Extension to be invoked accordingly.     Apple documentation recommends using the in-app webview class {{ASWebAuthenticationSession}} for web based login authentication, but {{ASWebAuthenticationSession}} does not allow the extension.     This should be resolved with the new plugin functionality in iOS 13, but until then we would need to adopt {{SFSafariViewController}}."

summary: Extension point to filter information about created pages on Confluence dashboard
description: "Hi Team!    My name's <USER>and I'm a product owner of [Smart Attachments for Confluence|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1219123/smart-attachments-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview] app.    It will be nice to have an extension point to filter appearance of pages on the Confluence dashboard. So that we will have an option to block appearance of newly created pages (created by some third-party app) on the dashboard.    At the moment we are setting the old dates to pages, so they do not appear. But this is not convenient and is not good in general from the architecture perspective.    *Use Case*    We are using your native Confluence pages to render our own entities (folders) in the space storage for keeping documents and files. The problem is that a newly created page is displayed on Confluence dashboard. We would greatly appreciate if you could provide/implement some extension point to filter information about created pages on Confluence dashboard. So that we can use the native functionality instead of tricking Confluence with dates."

summary: Extended configuration of the xrootd services in Qserv
description: "h1. Goals  This effort covers improvements to be made in Qserv to allow additional tunings of the XRootD/SSI parameters for better performance and scalability of Qserv.    h1. The improvements  h2. ""spread""    In the initial implementation of the ticket a configuration parameter for the XRootD's ""spread"" will be added. Specifically, this will require:  * Adding an optional parameter for a value of the desired ""spread"" into the configuration file of the Qserv {{czar}} component.  * Fetching this parameter from the configuration, and if found - configuring XRootD by calling method {{XrdSsiProvider::SetSpread()}} before accepting or processing any user queries.  * Reporting this at the INFO level of the LSST Logger.  * Modifying the corresponding configuration template/file which is used for generating Qserv deployments (bare OS (single node or multi node) Docker-based, or Kubernetes-based).  * Testing the effect of various values of the ""spread"" in the large scale Qserv deployment at NCSA (the ""development"" cluster) for many queries launched in parallel against catalogs partitioned for 150,000 chunks.  * Reporting results of the testing in the downstream comments of this ticket.    A value of the parameter is set in the configuration file *{{/qserv/run/etc/qserv-czar.cnf}}*:      h2. The XRD_PARALLELEVTLOOP optimization  The parameter sets a number of the parallel threads in the XRootD event processing loop. Setting this parameter to some reasonably high number has been proven to improve the ""depth"" of the ""in-flight"" requests queue which could be efficiently processed by the XRootD client stack before seeing a significant ""exponential"" degradation in the performance.    In the current implementation of the XRootD API (version 4), the only way to pass this parameter to the client stack is via the environment variable {{XRD_PARALLELEVTLOOP}}. The latest version of the API (5) would allow setting a value of the parameter programmatically. Therefore, the only option to use this parameter in Qserv is by setting the variable to a desired value in the startup script of the {{mysql-proxy}} service before starting the process.    The experimentally devised value of the parameter (before seeing no benefits in increasing its value) in multiple tests was:    "

summary: Add support for the secondary index in sph-partition
description: "h1. 1. Objectives    A goal of this effort is to add support for the _secondary index_ when partitioning data of the _child_ (_dependent_) tables. Here is the extended partitioning ""protocol"":   # the partitioning is done based on either *{{RA}}*/*{{DEC}}* of a table, or a precomputed map *{{id -> (chunk,sub-chunk)}}*   # the *{{RA}}*/*{{DEC}}* mode is triggered by a presence of the following config or command line parameter *{{part.pos=<ra_column_name>,<dec_column_name>}}*   # to trigger an alternative partitioning method one would have to eliminate *{{part.pos}}* from the configuration and provide *{{part.id=<fk-to-director-table>}}*, where the ""foreign"" key specified in the last parameter would be is the name of the corresponding column in the child table.   #* the partitioning of a dependent table will fail if an object identifier won't be found in the map   # if both *{{part.pos}}* and *{{part.id}}* are present then the former takes precedence over the later   # the *{{id -> (chunk,sub-chunk)}}* map gets created gets created if both *{{part.pos}}* and *{{part.id}}* are present. The map is stored in a file named *{{index.txt}}* at the same output destination where other partitioning products (chunks, overlaps) are stored.   # the *{{id -> (chunk,sub-chunk)}}* map files produced by the partitioner will be the {{CSV}}-formatted files following an output _dialect_ specified in a collection of parameters *{{part.out_csv.}}**  #* if the file already exist it will be open in the append mode, so that new object entries will be added to the file.   # if only *{{part.id}}* is present (indirect partitioning) then parameter *{{part.id-url=file:<path>/chunk_object_index.txt}}* is required by the partitioner   ** the partitioning of a dependent table will fail if no valid map will be provided   # the overlap tables are generated ONLY in the *{{RA}}*/*{{DEC}}* partitioning mode AND if *{{part.overlap != 0}}*   # the *{{id -> (chunk,sub-chunk)}}* map would *NEVER* be generated for the entries put into the overlap tables   #* that won't be needed because the _child_ (_dependent_) tables do not have overlaps in Qserv.    Other improvements:  * add partitioning option *{{part.disable-chunks={0|1}}}* to disable writing output chunked products. This mode would be used in the _dry run_ mode for evaluating input files, or for generating the _secondary_ index files for object tables which have been partitioned/ingested.    h1. 2. Design considerations and Requirements    In the initial (simplest) file-based implementation, the index files will be the regular *{{CSV}}* text files. Each line of the files will have the following format (assuming the _comma_-separated output formatting):    Where:   * there will be exactly one entry for each object encountered in the _director_ table's data file(s)   * values in each row of the file will be separated by a separator specified in the corresponding configuration parameters    h2. 2.1 Other notes/requirements   * consider using abstract interfaces for fetching data from the output index.   * consider implementing plugins for the abstract interfaces. Each such plugin would be mapped to the corresponding implementation of the persistent (or service-based) secondary index (examples: file-based, MySQL-based, REST API-based, Redis-based, etc.)"

summary: Add Amazon S3 Sink connector to Kafka-connect-manager
description: "The [Amazon S3 sink connector|https://docs.confluent.io/current/connect/kafka-connect-s3/index.html] is released under the Confluent Community License.    It presents all features required to use with the {{kafka-aggregator}} like time-based partitioners, schema evolution, and support to write Parquet format on AWS S3 and Non-AWS storage providers.      With the refactoring of {{kafka-connect-manager}} in DM-25488 in this ticket we'll the configuration class for the Amazon S3 Sink connector and the CLI interface to configure this connector.   "

summary: Add Kafka serialization wrapper code to lsst.alert.stream
description: "Add the code for writing and reading alert packets into Kafka to lsst.alert.stream, since that package is distributed on PyPI already. This code will be used by community broker authors."

summary: Metrics and Telemetry infrastructure
description: "This epic captures work in two of our core metrics and telemetry systems, SQuaSH and EFD, as well as related ArgoCD and Kafka work.     It includes:    * A sprint resolving technical debt on the SQuaSH APIs, including the ability to ship metrics from different environments (this is currently dealt with manually)  * A continuation of the aggregation work  * Input to the Observatory Logging project   * Support for Commissioning and Integration activities as requested, including training  "

summary: Redeploy Kafka on the NCSA test stand and investigations after the cluster intervention
description: "On May 28th there was maintenance on the NCSA test stand cluster to replace weave CNI plugin by flannel.    I have restarted the EFD services on May 28th and it seemed to be working for some CSCs.    After starting all the CSCs we saw errors like:        That means the schema for a given topic was not registered when the producer started, or that it was registered with a different schema ID than the one used in the serialized message for that topic.    As a conseguence the connector tasks could not start properly and failed with the error above.    It looks like Kafka and the Schema Registry got to an inconsistent state and I suspect that happened because of network instability (?) as the producers and the EFD were running during the intervention.    Anyway, to fix that we stoped the producers and manually deleted that topic from Kafka and the corresponding schema from the registry.        Then we saw the problem for another topic, and repetitively to other topics. I only got the EFD working again after a fresh start i.e. redeploying Kafka removing all the previous topics and schemas :(        "

summary: Extend the Replication/Ingest framework and REST API with algorithms and tools for indexing catalogs
description: "The current implementation of the Replication/Ingest doesn't provide any mechanism for managing (inspecting, creating or dropping) indexes on the data tables within Qserv. Only a few essential (for Qserv to function) indexes get automatically created. A lack of indexes may affect a performance of many user queries. Hence a goal of this development is to add the following capabilities to the system:  * extending the Replication Framework with a set of _request_ and _job_ classes for managing indexes at workers.  * adding a command-line version of the Replication _controller_ tool for managing the indexes  * extending the REST services of the *Master Replication Controller* with the index management services    Other notes:  * the REST services need to be protected with the _auth_key_ mechanism as explained in: https://jira.lsstcorp.org/browse/DM-22745  * it may be necessary to consider putting a cap on the number of indexes which were created by the system due to disk space constraints at a particular Qserv deployment. For example, the system may need to evaluate the amount of the available disk space on the coresponding file system (at Qserv workers) before attempting to create a new index.  "

summary: Support host-level OAuth authentication provider configuration
description: "DNN installations with many sites sometimes want to share the API secret & key between those sites, instead of having to redefine them for each site."

summary: DCC - Architectural Review
description: "The goal of this task is perform an architectural review of DCC Architecture.    *DB Schema*:  # Ensure proper indexes are in place  # Ensure system will not create overly large tables, thus requiring lots of disk space  # Queries are efficient and combined together    *Caching*:  # We have decent caching so that we are not hitting database for every page load (anonymous and registered users both)    *Client Side optimization*  # Ensure client side JS is optimal.    In case of detection of any issues a new jiras will be created and link to this task."

In an Install or Upgrade package there should only be the minimum assemblies needed to install/upgrade.  All other asemblies will be deployed by the Installer if necessary.    The following should not be copied to the bin in the package:    * Dnn.Modules.*** - any module with this namespace  * DNNConnect.CKEditorProvider.dll  * DotNetNuke.Modules.*** - any module in this namespace  * DotNetOpenAuth.**  * OAuth.**  * DotNetNuke.Authentication.**  * *.designer.cs  * *.less"

summary: Implement function that removes KafkaBrokers value
description: "As a consumer of the fabric config API I would like to call a function during my migration from Kafka to EtcdRaft to remove the KafkaBrokers section from my config.    The KafkaBrokers section should normally not be used if the consenter type is etcdraft but this function enables us to support cleaning up migrated configs.    *Acceptance*    I can call a function to entirely remove KafkaBrokers similar to the API function for removing top level orderer addresses. As example is also added/improved for demonstrating how to migrate from Kafka to EtcdRaft."

summary: Channel participation API - Join, Leave & List channels w/o a system channel
description: "Currently in order to create a channel one needs to issue a config transaction to the system channel. This has *privacy, scalability* and *operational* disadvantages, since the system channel (and the orderers running it) is aware of all channels and of all channel members (at creation time).    In this feature we propose to expose a ""channel participation"" API that would allow a local orderer administrator to *join* and *leave* a channel, as well as to *list* all the channels that the *local orderer* is part of. This allows a Fabric network to be operated *without* the use of *a system channel*, which improves the privacy and scalability of the ordering service and the network as a whole. It also alleviates some of the operational problems associated with the current style of channel creation.    This feature is described in an RFC titled ""Channel participation api without system channel"" ([currently PR #24|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric-rfcs/pull/24]).    This feature was presented in a [Fabric contributor's meeting|https://wiki.hyperledger.org/display/fabric/Contributor+Meetings+2020] on April 1st 2020, the presentation is here: [^Channel Participation API - community call - 1Apr2020.pdf].     "

summary: Kafka to Raft consensus-type migration
description: "Migrate an existing kafka-based ordering service to a raft-based ordering service.      This epic includes:   * Design & implement the mechanism in the orderer node   * Provide tools that automate the process   * Provide documentation and guidelines    Design document: [Google Drive|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1I7zWBomEPsgxgDYiYwE7ZPaT_Htll996nM2Y5A4xY-8/edit?usp=sharing], or see attached PDF.    Revised design of previous task: https://jira.hyperledger.org/browse/FAB-12777"

summary: Raft Consensus - System Test Ideas Incubator
description: "This is the proving ground to capture ideas for upcoming raft system tests areas. Once we harden these ideas and agree to create actual testcases, they should be linked instead to:   * FAB-13538 ""Raft Consensus - System Test""         Below is a preliminary list. (I created Jira testtasks placeholders for each of these, but they each should be reviewed carefully and expanded into multiple testcases.)  h3. Functional and Negative Test Areas:   * Targeted orderer restarts during onboarding   * kube-monkey: random restarts during traffic runs with more channels and concurrency   * Quorum tests: stop multiple orderers(some or all) to prevent quorum, and then recover them in different sequences, with traffic   * Migration – all tests   * TLS cert rotation for all orderersand onboard new ordererafterwards   * TLS cert rotation for one or more orderers, with traffic, with restarts   * Configure OSNs in channels with only partly overlapping consenter sets (e.g. A,B,C vs B,C,D) and run traffic   * With overlapping consenter sets, also rerun tests for restarts, onboarding, cert rotation (as needed)   * Configure OSNsindifferentordererorganizations and run traffic   * With OSNs in different orgs, also rerun restarts, onboarding, cert rotation (as needed)    h3. Scaling and Performance:   * Performance: Raft vs Kafka   * Scaling traffic runs on more orderers, e.g., 5, 7, 11, 15, 100   * Higher traffic rates using HFRD   * Higher traffic rates (orderersonly) using OTE    h3. Stress and Disruption:   * Resend transactions - after timeout of single TXs when needed   * Resend transactions - DoSscenarios   * Flood orderersto create stress, some while restarting orderers(e.g. thundering herd)   * Send duplicate transactions to multiple orderers(IT or SVT?)   * Other stress and disruptive test ideas…         "

summary: Support for IMAP/POP OAuth 2.0 token based auth
description: "We need to support IMAP/POP OAuth 2.0 token based auth:    Accordingly to this [post|https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/Exchange-Team-Blog/Improving-Security-Together/ba-p/805892] in the Microsoft Tech Community post, Microsoft will stop to support basic authentication for IMAP and POP protocols for Exchange Online by Oct/2020. The only way to authenticate in Exchange Online service for POP/IMAP protocols will be through OAuth 2.0 token based auth.    It may affect our customers that have IMAP/POP Jira Cloud external Mail Handlers configured pointing to this service."

summary: Load balancer status URL improvements
description: "Having a ""/status"" endpoint for load balancer health checks is good, but some simple enhancements could make load balancer integration much more functional.  I can see two compelling use cases:  1. When shutting down one cluster member for maintenance/upgrade:  As of today (8.3/4.3), the minimally disruptive way to shut down one cluster node is   -- Go to load balancer, disable node1   -- Shut down Jira on node 1   -- Start up Jira on node 1   -- Go to load balancer, re-enable node1   and repeat for node 2 in (say) a rolling upgrade.     If Jira would change the return code from /status to (e.g.) ""STOPPING"" for a small period of time before shutting down, the manual interaction with the load balancer could be avoided. The shutdown delay would need to be tunable (e.g. via a property), so customers could match behaviour with the load balancer health check interval.    2. When performing an offline re-index.    The post-upgrade procedure for a rolling upgrade suggests that you should:  -- Remove one node from the load-balancer  -- Perform an offline index  -- Add the node back in to the load-balancer  -- Copy the resulting index to the other nodes    Now, this is /possible/ by adding additional listeners to server.xml and connecting directly to the target host, but when combined with SAML login it starts to require serious gymnastics.      It could be done automatically, however, by adding a 'cluster-aware offline rebuild' mode that :  -- set /status to return (e.g.) ""OFFLINE""  -- wait long enough for the load balancer to notice  -- perform the offline rebuild  -- set /status to return ""RUNNING"" again  -- copy resulting index to the other nodes.    Regards,"

summary: REST API interface to manipulate priorities in Jira
description: "h3. Problem definition  Currently, users can create new priorities and link them to icons only via user interface. However, there is no published REST API endpoint that would allow user to:  * Add new / modify existing priorities and priority schemes  * Link icon's URL and change the existing one  ** icon URLs can be modified directly through database (priority table), but this still requires a restart and also, introduces risk of data corruption.    h3. Suggested resolution  Implement and expose REST API interface that would enable users to manipulate priorities without the need do it through user interface."

summary: Ensure that Default Group Membership works with third-party SSO

Problem Definition  When configuring Jira to use SSO with third-party tools such as Jira SAML SSO using default group memberships, Jira does not populate its tables to include all related data to ensure that users can log in properly for the first time. Due to that, it is necessary to manually insert each user in the required group on Jira to ensure that they will be able to log in.      h3. Suggested Solution  Detect when an user tries to log in using SSO to include the necessary data in the *cwd* tables in the first login to ensure that he/she will receive the group membership properly and will be able to access Jira."

But if they try using:      Then instead of being redirected to SAML and getting back to the dashboard, the users are presented with an error in Jira:    !Screen Shot 2019-05-17 at 15.27.34.png|thumbnail!    It would be good if instead of this, users were just authenticated and able to see the Dashboard."

summary: Add REST endpoint to manage Issue Dev Details (git repo, deployment and build data) like Jira Cloud
description: "As a Microsoft Partner, our company gets free access to TFS / Azure DevOps and use it for source code management, builds and deployments. We've been using Jira server for quite a long time but never quite integrated TFS data into Jira Issues the way it should (ie just like Bitbucket and Bamboo do):    !image-2019-05-15-10-29-46-840.png|width=237,height=204!    Upon reading [this article|[https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-articles/6-integrations-every-Jira-Software-Cloud-team-NEEDS-to-know/ba-p/1069040]] and looking at the Rest API for Jira Cloud, it seems it would be feasible to do just that. Unfortunately, the following API are only available for Jira Cloud:   * [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/rest/#api-group-Development-Information]   * [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/rest/#api-group-Deployments]   * [https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/rest/#api-group-Builds]    We think it should also be ported / made available in Jira Server as well to enable custom integration."

summary: Custom Field Value cache can grow indefinitely
description: "h3. Problem Definition  {{EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister}} caches all Custom Field values related to specific Issue from {{customfieldvalue}} table in _request-scope_ cache.    This caching was introduced as a performance optimisation to speed up issue indexing.   Probably it also helps in ViewIssue, issue-nav's table view and sorting by custom field through a database.    However, currently this cache can grow indefinitely and this can cause high memory pressure.    h3. Suggested Solution  Introduce some limits to the cache size, I.e. 1/10/100 issues only    h3. Notes  In the following example, we have cached values for 62k issues. (each having ~50 cf values)  !cf-request-cache.png|width=362,height=302,thumbnail!    h3. Notes on fix  Copy from [focusedCommentId=1972882|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66555?focusedCommentId=1972882&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-1972882]    h4. Background  In Jira many custom field values are read from DB. These values are often read in a loop, one by one. To minimise the number of DB calls, when one custom field value is read, {{EagerLoadingOfBizCustomFieldPersister}} reads and caches all of the values for the given issue. This is a request-scoped cache, so as soon as one Jira page is displayed, it's binned.    h4. Problem  In some cases the request scope lasts for long time. This is the case for example for CSV export, where all issues are exported in one go. The cache keeps on accumulating custom field values for longer than needed - as soon as an issue has been written out, it won't be accessed any more. There's no point in keeping it in memory. Yet, until this fix, we did that.    Another example (which is not really a real web request) is when a batch of issues is indexed. As soon as one issue has been indexed, we don't need to keep it in the memory. For this we had a workaround of cleaning the cache manually, which is redundant now.    h4. Fix summary  *We changed the custom field cache from unbounded to size 10 (LRU)*. In most cases size 1 would be enough, but in some scenarios, e.g. reading a subtask and its parent, a slightly bigger cache will be beneficial.     h4. Fix performance  I tested CSV export of 100K issues, each having 10 custom field values. Before the fix the request cache would have ~1945MB at the end of the export, now it has ~57MB.    Running our [JPT|https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/jira-performance-tests] showed no performance regression. Indexing speed wasn't impacted either."

summary: Support v1 API over gRPC.
description: "Supporting v1 API over gRPC would make it easier for people to adapt the v1 API, and Mesos in general:  1. The current HTTP API is not easy to use.  2. gRPC is a popular RPC framework.  3. gRPC can support different language bindings.    Since v1 API is based on proto2, the plan is to declare gRPC services in proto3 but uses the existing proto2 messages."

summary: Transition libprocess on Windows to use the Thread Pool API
description: "This epic covers the work necessary to use the Windows [Thread Pool API|https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/ms686766(v=vs.85).aspx] instead of libev or libevent on Windows.    The design of libprocess closely mimics the built-in Thread Pool API, using I/O Completion Ports for asynchronous I/O. It makes sense to use this API because the semantics of libprocess map 1-1 to the Windows async asynchronous support.    Transitioning to IOCP is necessary to implement non-blocking pipes, as the existing eventing libraries (e.g. libevent) only implement asynchronous sockets on Windows; but there are many instances of Mesos requiring asynchronous pipes too. As such, the existing use of libevent is fundamentally broken.    Specifically, this was first discovered in test {{HungDockerTest.ROOT_DOCKER_InspectHungDuringPull}}, but is also evidenced through {{IOTest.Read}} and others which use non-blocking pipes.    The alternative eventing libraries libev and libuv were considered, but we are choosing not to use them as they do not implement non-blocking pipes sufficiently for Windows. libev is not easy to build on Windows, and does not support IOCP. While libevent uses IOCP for reading and writing sockets, it uses {{select()}} to detect and dispatch events (which results in poor performance), and does not use support IOCP for pipes. libuv claims to support IOCP on Windows, but they do not support [named pipes|https://github.com/libuv/libuv/issues/1688] and only supportes [synchronous pipes|https://github.com/libuv/libuv/issues/95]. Moreover, it is simpler to use the Thread Pool API in libprocess directly, instead of using a library to abstract over that API, as libprocess already provides the abstraction we're seeking for Mesos.    Additionally, by using the built-in Windows IOCP support, we can eventually support SSL using the Windows crypto libraries, instead of requiring OpenSSL on Windows (the current case for libprocess)."

We can use the same JWT-based authN/authZ mechanism for executors, where the agent will create a auth token for each local resource provider instance:  "

Later, we could update the build system to allow Mesos to use executor authentication when built without libevent/SSL."
