summary: Add cluster support for API-Gateway policies
description: "As Hazelcast will not be exposed to the policies, we need to add to the Policies' Context, a distributed TimeSupplier, OS and ClusterManager"

summary: SDK Operation parameter with defaultValue "attributesMediaType" is populated with payload media type instead
description: "If you define a SDK operation with a parameter that defines its default value as #[attributesMediaType], during execution it is populated with Payload MediaType instead of Attributes MediaType.    The reason for this is that when operation's parameters are resolved in class org.mule.runtime.module.extension.internal.runtime.execution.MethodArgumentResolverDelegate, if the parameter type is org.mule.runtime.api.metadata.MediaType it automatically uses the MediaTypeArgumentResolver, and this resolver always ends up using the Payload MediaType"

summary: SDK: Inject connection on PagingProvider#close() method
description: "Currently the connection is not being injected in the PagingProvider#close(), it would be ideal to have it in case the user needs it to perfom something when closing the PagingProvider.     the email connector for example would take advantage of this feature"

summary: Define a DslSyntaxResolver API on Tooling
description: "As part of the clean up on Tooling to remove mule-api a new API and service has to be declared in order to allow getting the DslSyntax for a operation/source/parameter/parameterGroup."

summary: Connector Migration tool and document from Mule 3.x to Mule 4
description: "We need to make the connector migration tool and document from Mule 3.x to Mule 4 for customers and partners who built connectors for Mule 3.x     "

summary: Add privileged API for plugin artifacts
description: "There are scenarios, where a plugin A must be extended on another plugin B, and to be able to do it, iB must use some A classes that makes no sense to be available on the A's public API.    To avoid exposing those classes, will be better to have a privileged API that the plugin A can define to enable extension on those scenarios. Similar to what is supported on container's modules."

summary: Move/migrate uses of component to compatibility module
description: "This is to move <component> and it's dependencies like:   - <*-entry-point-resolver>   - <spring-object> // This one may require integration the new spring module   - <singleton-object>    - <prototype-object>   - etc.    "

summary: Rethink how smart connector must work with the component model framework
description: "Rethink how smart connector must work with the component model framework"

summary: Remove org.apache.commons from exported packages
description: "Core module is exporting many packages from org.apache.commons, mainly from Utils classes that we are extending in Mule.  Most of the functionality is used only inside the container, what is used externally (in plugins for example) is the most common utilities, like StringUtils.isEmpty and stuff like that.  The proposal to hide those packages is to provide only a subset of the utility classes in the API. Those classes should not extend/expose apache commons classes. The utilities subset must contain only the most used utility methods.  When an artifact (service/plugin/etc) requires an utility method that is not part of the API, that artifact must add the required third party library. (ie: apache commons)"

summary: Update /beta/repositories and /beta/repositories/*/* endpoints to /v1
description: "We want to update /<USER>repositories endpoints to /v1/repositories endpoints so customers can adopt and use these endpoints without worrying about the stability stigma from having a <USER>label.    *Acceptance Criteria*   # Update */<USER>repositories* to */v1/repositorySettings* and ensure both /<USER>and /v1 prefixes can access the same API logic.   # Update the */<USER>repositories/***/** REST APIs to */v1/repositories/***/** and ensure both  /<USER>and /v1 prefixes access the same API logic.   # /<USER>APIs are hidden from Swagger UI    *Notes*   * We are not changing the REST API endpoint /v1/repositories. This endpoint lists repositories and is intended to be used by CI/CD tools like Jenkins. Not changing this endpoint to ensure we don't impact customer's existing CI/CD pipeline."

summary: Add "Local" as a synonym to "default" for the source attribute in the REST API endpoints
description: "Currently, when the API endpoint for managing NXRM users is supplied with a source attribute value of ""Local"" or ""local"", it does not recognize the value. Since the UI uses ""Local"" as the source, we should allow the endpoints to use the same string in an attempt to improve developer experience of our REST API endpoints."

summary: Allow REST API search and download through proxy repositories
description: "Currently a REST API search or download request against a proxy repository will only work if the components are cached locally in a proxy repository.     For instance a request to download the latest snapshot jar for a GAV:    This will fail unless the jar is already downloaded into the proxy.    Retrieving the latest version of a component via REST API through a proxy is a use case we should be supporting.  Possible implementations for this include implementing a remote search capability, or, specifically for Maven, using the maven-metadata.xml files to find available versions."

summary: SAML - Handle user logout
description: "SAML supports logout services. When configured, NXRM should support and issue logouts to the IdP.    *Acceptance Criteria*    When a user logs out through NXRM and the IdP metadata is configured with a logout service, then the user will be logged out of NXRM and the IdP."

summary: setting tags in URL for uploading via maven/gradle/ivy
description: "The REST API provides POST request for the component API with parameters to set tag during upload. Customer would prefer a way to craft the URL to include the tag info so that a maven/gradle/ivy upload would apply the tag implicitly.     "

summary: Allow to specify the number of items per page when using the REST API
description: "When using the Nexus API to get lists of assets and components (-or any other endpoint that supports pagination-) the result set is limited to 10 items per page.    https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-public/blob/master/components/nexus-repository/src/main/java/org/sonatype/nexus/repository/rest/internal/resources/AssetsResource.java#L107  https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-public/blob/master/components/nexus-repository/src/main/java/org/sonatype/nexus/repository/rest/internal/resources/ComponentsResource.java#L140    This means that when using the Nexus API to query large repos the interaction is rather slow since a new request has to go back and forth for every 10 items queried. If we could customize this value and increase it, I believe this would greatly improve performance.    The /search resource is limited to 50 items per page:    https://github.com/sonatype/nexus-public/blob/master/components/nexus-repository/src/main/java/org/sonatype/nexus/repository/rest/internal/resources/SearchResource.java#L104      h4. Expected    - expose a parameter+value that the caller can specify on request that can customize the number of top level items to return per page in the response  - a REST resource that accepts this parameter must also have a documented <USER>coded maximum value that the passed parameter value will not be able to exceed ( to protect the server from abuse )  - if the specified value is larger than the <USER>coded maximum value allowed for that resource, then use the hardcoded maximum value instead of erroring - document this behavior and the maximum value so if the user wonders why they don't get more per page, they can learn why"

summary: add REST API for creating tasks
description: "Users want a REST API to create scheduled tasks. In particular Admin related tasks that are recommended for any NXRM installation, like Export database task."

summary: Nexus REST API to download artifacts with classifier
description: "Depending on the build, an artifact exists in Nexus sometimes with a classifier and sometimes without a classifier. If the latest snapshot version has a classifier and the REST api is used to download the artifact WITHOUT providing the classifier, I am expecting Nexus to give the artifact WITHOUT the classifier from the previous snapshot version. Instead Nexus gives me the latest snapshot version WITH the classifier. maven-dependency-plugin:get is correctly fetching the artifact    Also, when an artifact exists with and without a classifier on the same snapshot version, downloading the one WITHOUT the classifier downloads the one WITH the classifier"

summary: add privileges for controlling actions against REST endpoints
description: "There are no privileges controlling access for each specific REST endpoint other than nx-all. But if I just want to allow access to certain REST endpoints to a user, giving them nx-all ( admin) level access is too permissive.    As one customer describes for the Search REST API:    {quote}...we are looking to have this feature to be well controlled.   For example, users may use the API search/download feature without the admin's knowledge causing a performance issue. On a repositories such as Maven Groups, this could be a serious concern.{quote}    "

summary: Add REST API to find old user tokens by realm
description: "Customers want to expire old user tokens by the realm they are associated with. See NEXUS-16690    NXRM 3.17.0 added a <USER>API to delete user tokens (NEXUS-19170), but an admin still has no REST way to find the tokens to delete by age.    There should be a REST endpoint to find user tokens by realm and older than a certain age. return the userid, realm, and created date.    It would be expected the user then uses the response to determine when to call the DELETE API for NEXUS-16690.  "

summary: Add REST API to Reset User Token
description: "In order to facilitate administrating NXRM we need a REST endpoint which can be used to reset the user token for a given user."

summary: Audit trail for staging move/promotion of a component
description: "Current v3.15.x.. gives a confirmation about the movement of a component using the REST API.. but return response is missing with Audit trail information as.. what is the timestamp of the component when that was built first time..? to which repository? Timestamp when component moved from one repository to another..?    If we have this component information available over API.. this will help to create Reports in much cleaner way."

summary: Add REST API Endpoint for Content Selector CRUD
description: "In order to support managing teams we need to be able to support CRUD operations for Content Selectors."

summary: Android: Update toLocale*String() methods to support locale/options
description: "*Summary:*  On Android, the following methods ignore the {{locales}} and {{options}} arguments. They also ignore the current locale and always use {{""en-US""}} when formatting a string.  * [Date.toLocaleString()|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toLocaleString]  * [Date.toLocaleDateString()|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toLocaleDateString]  * [Date.toLocaleTimeString()|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toLocaleTimeString]  * [Number.toLocaleString()|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number/toLocaleString]  * [String.localeCompare()|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String/localeCompare]  * [String.toLocaleLowerCase()|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String/toLocaleLowerCase]  * [String.toLocaleUpperCase()|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String/toLocaleLowerCase]    On iOS, the above methods *+do+* support locale/options.  We should add the same on Android for parity and to better support the standard JavaScript APIs.    *Note:*  To add official {{Intl}} support to V8, we would have to {{#if}} it in, but this involves adding the ICU (International Components for Unicode) C++ library which is about 20 MB per architecture. This is too big and is not mobile friendly. Especially since Google Play has a 100 MB limit on APKs. So, the most reasonable solution is to implement this ourselves in Java.    *Alternatives:*  On older Titanium versions, your only option to generate localized strings was via APIs:  * [String.formatDecimal()|https://docs.<USER>com/platform/latest/#!/api/Global.String-method-formatDecimal]  * [String.formatDate()|https://docs.<USER>com/platform/latest/#!/api/Global.String-method-formatDate]  * [String.formatTime()|https://docs.<USER>com/platform/latest/#!/api/Global.String-method-formatTime]  "

summary: Android: Add Intl.Collator support
description: "*Summary:*  On Android, we should add support for the JavaScript {{Intl.Collator}} class.  https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Intl/Collator    A collator is used to do localized string comparisons. This allows an app to correct sort text for the current locale/language.    This is already supported on iOS. We should add it to Android for parity.    *Note:*  To add official {{Intl}} support to V8, we would have to {{#if}} it in, but this involves adding the ICU (International Components for Unicode) C++ library which is about 20 MB per architecture. This is too big and is not mobile friendly. Especially since Google Play has a 100 MB limit on APKs. So, the most reasonable solution is to implement this ourselves in Java.  "

summary: Webpack: Tap into hooks before/after other plugins
description: "For TIMOB-27858, plugins need the ability to tap into hooks before/after other plugins to ensure the order in which certain aspects of a plugin gets applied. This is especially important for {{chainWebpack}} to make sure the base configuration is present before other third-party plugins may try to modify it.    *Default behavior*    By default plugins are evaluated in series. Built-in plugins from appcd-plugin-webpack are always loaded first. Plugins from {{package.json}} are loaded in the order they are defined there.    *API proposal*    Add {{options}} argument as the last argument with {{after}}/{{before}} properties to control the order in which values will be applied.    {code:js}  module.exports = function (api, options) {    api.chainWebpack(      config => {        config   .entry('main')   .add('./src/main.js')   .end();      },      { after: 'built-in:config/prod' }    );  };  {code}"

summary: Android: Build "analytics" module with gradle
description: "Module needs to be rebuilt for Titanium 9.0.0 to support the following:  * Built as an Android ""AAR"" library.  * Provide a ""POM"" file defining its dependencies. (Resolves library version conflicts.)  * Support AndroidX libraries.  * Add x86_64 architecture support.    Repository:  https://github.com/<USER>aps_sdk  "

summary: Companion should have an option to disable running local websocket server
description: "Companion should have a config or install option to disable backwards compatible code paths. In particular, if users are using Confluence 7.3 + then they do not require Companion to start a local websocket server."

summary: Suggest a REST API method to get results for clustering monitoring.
description: "h3. Problem Definition    Currently, the Confluence admin cannot monitor the nodes in the Datacenter cluster using the REST API.  h3. Suggested Solution    Create REST API call methods: [Confluence REST API Documentation|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/REST/6.6.0/]   * Status of the Confluence cluster instead of checking every node associated with the cluster as we have in Bitbucket.   * Automatically send notifications on node count info availability when the node gets disconnected from a cluster (Confluence Cluster View) AND/OR   ** https://<base-url>/plugins/servlet/cluster-monitoring   * Automatically send notifications based on the unavailability of the node in a cluster (Confluence Cluster View)   ** http://<node-url>/status"

summary: SI: Azure connector should allow to specify a endpoint suffix
description: "Currently, we don't specify the endpoint when connecting to Azure account so it'll use default endpoint - ""blob.core.windows.net"". We should allow the user to specify the endpoint suffix or there will be error messages as the screenshot.     !schot1.png|width=1000! "

summary: SI: Oauth Cookies should be deleted / expired when session expires
description: "Currently if the expiration timeout for a DNN session occurs before an oauth token such as the FacebookUserToken, the user is auto logged in as soon as the login page is loaded since the oauth_token is still alive; nevertheless, the user should be prompted with the login page again as the session should be expired, and one should have the option to select a different login methods.    To reproduce:    1. Enable FB Login.  2. Login with FB  3. Wait for .DOTNETNUKE timeout to expire, do not enable persistentcookie  4. Go to login page.    Actual Result.  User is redirected to home page and auto-logged in with facebook    Expected Result.  The facebookusertoken cookie should be removed once the dotnetnuke cookie / persistent cookie expires."

summary: Separate Platform integration tests framework into its own dll
description: "To simplify writing tests by community contributors, we need to separate the test framework into its own DLL so it can be referenced and used by others in a simpler way."

summary: Allow login to JWT using user-email beside using the username
description: "The current implementation of JWT allows only the username to be used in login credentials. Some customers are willing to use user-email for this purpose.    We need to enhance (or fix) the provider to allow login using user-email as well. Of course, this should follow the setting in the system whether to use name or email for login.    Note: the setting under: {{PB > Security > Member Accounts > REGISTRATION SETTINGS > Use Email Address as Username}} is currently respected, but some users might need to use both simultaneously.    Update: this might not be an issue at all, and we need to discuss whether this is a good feature to implement or not."

summary: PB General: Lazy loading + caching
description: "The method call 'gettable' (GET /API/personaBar/localization/gettable?culture=en-US HTTP/1.1) needs to be cached. Lazy loading would be even better. I personally use 'Strathweb.CacheOutput' and it works fine. So cache the web api methods with Strathweb.CacheOutput, e.g., [CacheOutput(ClientTimeSpan = 3600, ServerTimeSpan = 3600)]."

summary: Connectors in Platform
description: "The Evoq Connectors need to be renamed and moved to Platform (the package type; not the specific connectors). As part of this, the word ""Evoq"" needs to be removed as part of it and the code for creating the connector package type needs to be re-located to the Platform. This should handle the upgrade scenarios for Evoq customers (so that the EvoqConnectors are now Connectors). summary: Add context for the super-transactions in the Qserv Ingest system
description: "A need in this improvement was triggered by an experience of ingesting catalogs into Qserv as per [DM-24587]. It was noticed that Qserv deployments within the Kubernetes  environment exhibit extra failure modes which are rarely seen within the ""traditional"" host-based static setups. This includes more frequent DNS failures (as Kubernetes has its own DNS server), more frequent connection losses to overloaded services, worker pods being restarted (and disappearing from the Kubernetes DNS), etc. All together this brings in a new spectrum of issues which may need to be handled by the clients workflows.    Here is some background on this subject. When the current Ingest system was being designed one of the main concerns to be addressed were failures seen when loading table contributions. That appeared as the highest risk area due to the large amount of data sent to servers as well as a high frequency of communications.  And for that, the super-transactions provide a reasonable protection. For instance, if a client loses a connection with a worker’s ingest server after a successful completion of some file ingest operation, and the client gets a connection error then it can always retry the “failed” operation within a new transaction. This is some kind of “false positive” scenario, which is generally harmless. When it comes to connection failures to the Master Replication Controller, then it all depends on a specific operation, and on its context. Some operations are safe to repeat. Others may require extra interactions with the Controller to figure out a state of a catalog. One example is the transaction management. If one sees a connection failure while waiting for a response from the REST service on a new transaction request it may not mean that the operation has failed _per se_. The transaction could have been successfully initiated. In this case a client would have to scan existing transactions to see which transactions are active and figure out which one (if any) was attempted by the client. Unfortunately, transactions don’t have an “owner”. All a client may learn from the Ingest system is that the name of a database associated with the transaction, a state of the transaction and the *{{start}}*/*{{stop}}* timestamps of the transaction. Based on these attributes of a transaction, it would be hard to figure out which one was initiated by a specific client. Altogether this may complicated a recovery process.    This development offers a solution to the last problem by allowing a transaction-requesting client to specify an optional context for a new transaction. It would be up to the client to formulate the context and send it to the Controller along with the transaction opening request. The context would be stored in the Controller’s database along with other info on the transaction. The context could be retrieved along with the transaction records via the earlier discussed service *{{GET /ingest/trans?database=<name>}}*. To make things more convenient here the context could be  specified as a JSON object sent with POST /ingest/trans. And it would be up to the client what to put into that object (though, it doesn’t  have to be huge). As an example, the context could include a host from which the transaction was requested, the PID of a process requested the transaction, or anything else. Some ingest workflows may even generate a UUID to be sent in the context so that the the workflow could implement a proper bookkeeping for this type of operations.    This development requires:  * extending the table schema for *{{qservReplica.transaction}}*  * extending a schema of a JSON object send with the *{{POST}}* request to *{{/ingest/trans}}* with an optional dictionary-type attribute *{{context}}*.  * extending an implementation of the *{{GET}}* request to *{{/ingest/trans}}* to return a context in attribute *{{context}}* for each transaction reported by the service.  * updating the documentation in the Ingest System: https://confluence.lsstcorp.org/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=133333850  * (possibly) increasing the version number reported by the *{{GET}}* service *{{/meta/version}}*.  "

summary: Release kafka-aggregator 0.1.0
description: "Initial release of kafka-aggregator with the following features:    * Use Faust windowing feature to aggregate a stream of messages.  * Use Faust-avro to add Avro serialization and Schema Registry support to Faust.  * Support to an internal Schema Registry to store schemas for the aggreated topics (optional).  * Create aggregation topic schemas from the source topic schemas and from the list of summary statistics to be computed.  * Ability to create Faust records dynamically from aggregation topic schemas.  * Ability to auto-generate code for the Faust agents (stream processors).  * Compute summary statistics for numeric fields: {{min()}}, {{mean()}}, {{median()}}, {{stdev()}}, {{max()}}.  * Add example module to initialize a number of source topics in kafka, control the number of fields in each topic, and produce messages for those topics at a given frequency.  * Use Kafdrop to inspect messages from source and aggregated topics.  * Add kafka-aggregator documentation site.  "

summary: Streamlined naming convention for the REST API resources in the Qserv Replication/Ingest System
description: "The current implementation of the REST API provided by the Qserv Replication/Ingest system for exploring and managing Qserv may need to be improved. Presently, the API provides two groups of resources:  {code:}  /replication/v1/<service>  /ingest/v1/<service>    /meta/version    In this case availability of services and their behavior will be completely driven by the current version of the services. Another benefit of this schema is that it would with documenting the REST API since each version of the documentation could refer to specific numbers returned by the proposed metadata service."

summary: Register aggregated topic schemas to a secondary schema registry
description: "In the EFD, data replication is done in one direction, copying kafka topics and schemas from a source cluster (e.g. base) to a destination cluster (e.g.  LDF) (see the multi Data Center [active-passive setup | https://docs.confluent.io/current/schema-registry/multidc.html])    The Aggregator is a component that runs on the destination cluster (e.g. LDF) and it creates new schemas for the aggregated topics.  In the active-passive setup, these schemas will exist only at the destination cluster, we don't want them replicated back to the source cluster. That means the schema IDs would no longer be globally unique if we use the same Schema Registry to register the aggregated topic schemas, there may be collisions between schema IDs for schemas created at the source and destination clusters.     For the Aggregator, we'll need *a secondary schema registry* to register the aggregated topic schemas.     In this ticket, we'll investigate this possibility further and configure the {{kafka-aggregator}} to read schemas for the source topics from the primary Schema Registry and write schemas to the secondary one.    "

summary: Disable browser caching of 403 errors
description: "If people go through CILogon but don't have the required groups, they get a 403 error from Gafaelfawr. Some browsers cache that error. Set the no-cache headers to try to prevent this so that retrying authentication once the user has been added to the appropriate group won't return a cached 403."

summary: Prototype a confluent schema registry instead of building our own registry
description: "Today, we manage schema versions by committing them in the sample-avro-alert repository, and we expect them to be on the local filesystem of the producer or consumer. We load all schemas into memory using a custom SchemaRegistry class in sample-avro-alert's Python utility code. alert_stream's code relies on this.    This setup probably won't survive in production. It depends too much on synchronizing consumers and producers by managing their local state - we'd have to send out an email telling people to please refresh files on their machines in order to take advantage of a schema update.    In addition, our SchemaRegistry implementation is somewhat buggy: it doesn't canonicalize schemas, so minor whitespace and documentation changes would be breaking (this is DM-24293).    One alternative seems to be the [Confluent Schema Registry|https://www.confluent.io/confluent-schema-registry/]. This is in use today at LSST by SQuARE; the kafkit project provides access to the schema registry API directly, and the confluent_kafka consumer/producer library supports transparent ser/de if a schema registry is available. It would handle canonicalization for us, as well as fingerprinting of schemas.    Output of this work would be a working set of Docker containers that runs a Schema Registry alongside a Kafka broker, and Python code that produces and consumes the alert stream using that data. A good demo would be to show an update to the schema. Output should also include a DMTN summarizing the work and the result."

summary: DBB Buffer Mngrs v1.1 Implementation
description: "﻿The project is nearing the time when it will want to automatically ingest raw images into a friendly-user Gen3 butler repository. The DBB Endpoint Buffer Manager should be modified to be capable of ingesting raw images into Gen2, Gen3, and Rucio. Raw images will continue to arrive on the DBB gateway node via both the DBB Handoff Buffer Manager as well as the rsync raw image transfer service. The ingests into Gen2, Gen3 and Rucio should be able to work with raw images from either service. This will deprecate the cron job running on the gateway node to ingest rsync'd data into gen2 repos. Ingests will be configurable to make softlinks instead of move file into place. During friendly-user time, Gen2 butler will still be the production mechanism to serve raw images. Problems with Rucio or Gen3 ingestion should not cause delays or problems with Gen2 ingestion. Also during this time, Gen3 will track the file in a posix datastore along with Rucio tracking the file for replications. An assumption is that the raw image should be moved into the final storage location before ingesting into Rucio or any butler repository. These mean that all ingestions (Rucio, Gen2, and Gen3) should be independent. This epic should not be dependent upon whether Rucio or Gen3 ingestion is ready for deployment. This epic should assume that python code will be provided that:    * Will take a filename (including path) to ingest  * Will take a dictionary of configuration value(s) (may end up including name of other config file)  * Will throw an exception on error (Actual interface to be determined by design and prototyping)    This ticket had been created automatically  Primavera ID: A7830"

summary: Create "kickdrum" app for Roundtable/SQuaRE Events
description: "The kickdrum app will regularly produce Kafka messages on Roundtable's SQuaRE Events service. Consumers can monitor this ""beat"" topic to ensure that they haven't silently lost a connection with a broker.    The reason for this issue seems to be [https://github.com/aio-libs/aiokafka/issues/575]    So we can either wait for that upstream issue to be resolved, or we can use kickdrum to work around it."

summary: Standardize Gen3 instrument class names and location
description: "Currently the gen3 instrument classes have no consistency in naming or module path. This adds to the confusion. Currently we have:    * lsst.obs.subaru.gen3.hsc.instrument.HyperSuprimeCam  * lsst.obs.decam.instrument.DarkEnergyCamera  * lsst.obs.lsst.gen3.instrument.LatissInstrument  * lsst.obs.lsst.gen3.instrument.Ts8Instrument  * lsst.obs.lsst.gen3.instrument.Ts3Instrument  * lsst.obs.lsst.gen3.instrument.UcdCamInstrument  * lsst.obs.lsst.gen3.instrument.PhosimInstrument  * lsst.obs.lsst.gen3.instrument.ImsimInstrument  * lsst.obs.lsst.gen3.instrument.LsstComCamInstrument  * lsst.obs.lsst.gen3.instrument.LsstCamInstrument    corresponding to instrument names of HSC, DECam, LATISS, LSST-TS8, LSST-TS3, LSST-UCDCam, PhoSim, LSST-ImSim, LSST-ComCam, lsstCam.    Instrument names have to be unique in Gen3 since telescope is not part of the data model. This is why TS8 is not TS8. lsstCam should probably be LSSTCam.    This ticket will try to clean things up a bit.    obs_cfht has no gen3 support at this time."

summary: Use context with timeout when CLIs connect to endorser/ordering endpoint
description: "Currently the only timeout that would prevent an unresponsive endpoint (ordering/endorsing) from stalling a CLI invocation forever is the long grpc connection timeout. We should instead supply a context with a timeout as part of the invocation, and expose a timeout."
